content:
# Working with Lists

**Object**

In general, anything that can be assigned to a variable in Python is referred to as an object.

*Strings, Integers, Floats, Lists etc.* are all objects.

Examples

-   "A"
-   1.25
-   [1,2,3]

## Identity of an Object

Whenever an object is created in Python, it will be given a unique identifier (id).

This unique id can be different for each time you run the program.

Every object that you use in a Python Program will be stored in Computer Memory.

The unique id will be related to the location where the object is stored in the Computer Memory.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/d8def327-1ad6-448e-9aac-d12eacd7bf8e.PNG)


### Finding Id

We can use the `id()` to find the id of a object.


#### Code

```python
print(id("Hello"))
```

#### Output

```

140589419285168
```

### Id of Lists

```python
list_a = [1, 2, 3]
list_b = [1, 2, 3]
print(id(list_a))
print(id(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
139637858236800
139637857505984
```

## Modifying Lists

### Modifying Lists - 1


When assigned an existing list both the variables `list_a` and `list_b` will be referring to the same object.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/7614082e-2bba-42e7-b4ff-fb8b5137cec5.PNG)

#### Code

```python
list_a = [1, 2, 3]
list_b = list_a
print(id(list_a))
print(id(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
140334087715264
140334087715264
```

### Modifying Lists - 2

When assigned an existing list both the variables `list_a` and `list_b` will be referring to the same object.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/8aaf5b5a-2ccd-42ae-ab6b-d083c8dc3276.PNG)


#### Code

```python
list_a = [1, 2, 3, 5]
list_b = list_a
list_b[3] = 4
print("list a : " + str(list_a))
print("list b : " + str(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
list a : [1, 2, 3, 4]
list b : [1, 2, 3, 4]
```

### Modifying Lists - 3

The assignment will update the reference to new object.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/70270ed1-dbc2-4319-a22d-2291baecd15b.PNG)

#### Code

```python
list_a = [1, 2]
list_b = list_a
list_a = [6, 7]
print("list a : " + str(list_a))
print("list b : " + str(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
list a : [6, 7]
list b : [1, 2]
```

### Modifying Lists - 4

The assignment will update the reference to a new object.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/bf7d1cc7-edbc-489f-adc2-3d08b98abe3e.PNG)

  
#### Code

```python
list_a = [1, 2]
list_b = list_a
list_a = list_a + [6, 7]
print("list a : " + str(list_a))
print("list b : " + str(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
list a : [1, 2, 6, 7]
list b : [1, 2]
```

### Modifying Lists - 5

Compound assignment will update the existing list instead of creating a new object.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/dea82eeb-2864-481d-8889-b4fdf0ce2822.PNG)

#### Code

```python
list_a = [1, 2]
list_b = list_a
list_a += [6, 7]
print("list a : " + str(list_a))
print("list b : " + str(list_b))
```

#### Output

```
list a : [1, 2, 6, 7]
list b : [1, 2, 6, 7]
```


### Modifying Lists - 6

Updating mutable objects will also effect the values in the list, as the reference is changed.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/4c44ea2e-adfe-4ae3-b87e-ffd7373fc9f7.jpg)

  
#### Code

```python
list_a = [1,2]
list_b = [3, list_a]
list_a[1] = 4
print(list_a)
print(list_b)
```

#### Output

```
[1, 4]
[3, [1, 4]]
```

### Modifying Lists - 7

Updating immutable objects will not effect the values in the list, as the reference will be changed.  

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/49b64a51-d85a-4b19-8742-96a00cf96621.jpg)


#### Code

```python
a = 2
list_a = [1,a]
print(list_a)
a = 3
print(list_a)
```

#### Output

```
[1, 2]
[1, 2]
```
